telltalePatterns: paragogic schwa insertion at coda consonants
lack of vowel reduction in unstressed syllables

prosody.intonation: L1-influenced with flat baseline and sudden pitch excursions on stressed syllables
prosody.fluencyNotes: Words produced in isolation with minimal catenation; paragogic vowel insertions disrupt connected speech.

strengths: Robust phonation with zero vocal fry
Accurate production of tense vowels like /u/ and /i/

recommendedFocus.reason: English requires unstressed syllables to reduce to schwa /ə/ or /ɪ/ for proper stress-timing; current syllable-timed production impedes natural rhythm.
Substitution with dental plosives creates functional load issues and potential minimal pair confusion.
Using an alveolar tap [ɾ] instead of [ɹ] heavily marks the speaker's L1 and distracts listeners during consonant clusters.

result.transcript: Yesterday the weather was really beautiful. So I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring is my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that, I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
